mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% provides room for rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% in a single month, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.0% is below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC over 5-day horizon is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate, indicating weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal growth rates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 value 23530.909 requires growth rate calculation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%, or Rank IC over 60-day horizon is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts remain above 1400 thousand units, indicating robust consumer demand and residential investment that jointly signal sustained economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST > 1400 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS 723853.0 and HOUST 1499.0 suggest strength
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines month-over-month while inflation measured by CPI accelerates above 3.5% annualized, indicating stagflationary conditions where supply constraints drive prices higher while demand weakens, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth > 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO 103.511 and CPIAUCSL 319.086 require trend analysis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of stagflationary episodes, or the joint effect of INDPRO decline and CPIAUCSL acceleration is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) in interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates to below 100 thousand persons per month while unemployment rate remains stable, indicating labor market slack emerging at the margin that signals reduced wage pressure and lower inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the Fed may pause rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100 thousand AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS 159069 and UNRATE 4.0% require momentum analysis
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where both PAYEMS and UNRATE conditions are met, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeds 4.75% while CPI inflation remains above 3.0%, indicating the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy in a high-inflation regime that suppresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS 4.33% is below trigger but CPIAUCSL trend relevant
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met, or the joint restrictive effect is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1300 thousand units for two consecutive months while retail sales growth turns negative, indicating synchronized contraction in both residential and consumer demand that signals imminent economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1300 thousand for 2 consecutive months AND RSAFS MoM growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST 1499.0 and RSAFS 723853.0 are above trigger thresholds
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient indicating joint demand weakness is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
